sourceMovie.title: Extraction
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Extraction for the same reason it broke through in 2020: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Sam Hargrave keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Expendables
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Extraction's action stayed with you, The Expendables (2010) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Extraction 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Extraction excels in action, Extraction 2 (2023) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Ransom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ransom (1996) is the natural next step after Extraction: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Monkey Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Extraction who respond to its action will find Monkey Man (2024) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Havoc
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Extraction fans keep landing on Havoc (2025) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Substitute
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Substitute draws from the same action well as Extraction, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1996 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Year of the Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Year of the Dragon (1985) captures the action appeal of Extraction without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts Year of the Dragon at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Cleaner
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Extraction — that tense edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Cleaner (2025). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: 6 Bullets
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind 6 Bullets (2012) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Extraction rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Pulp Fiction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Pulp Fiction (1994) is the kind of film Extraction fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The thriller overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Extraction?
faq.answer: Try The Expendables, Extraction 2, Ransom—each shares DNA with Extraction while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Extraction?
faq.answer: Extraction (2020) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Extraction part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Extraction Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
